rationale: ADR compliance + no-regression lens, all clear on merged branch @12c02de1. ADR-0037 (single decision home): internal/panel/gate.go has a ZERO-byte diff vs main; PanelGateDecision is untouched. R1 is genuinely CLI-layer — it swaps the FACTS gathered for a non-bead panel through the GateIO.RevParse seam (the leaf design ADR-0037 exists for) and sanitizeNonBeadDecision rewrites only d.Message (never d.Action), gated strictly on non-bead so bead panels, instruct, and complete read a byte-identical Decision. R3's Gate field is decision-inert, proven by TestPanel_GateFieldDecisionInertAllEnumValues across all five enum values. ADR-0035: invalid --gate is rejected via guard.NewFailure enumerating all five config.PanelGateKeys (single enum, no second copy); non-bead Block emits a genuine re-panel command, never a false `mindspec complete <bead>`. ADR-0040: L2 CLI made truthful, L3 workflow hardened without contract change. ADR-0030: one rev-parse through the executor seam, worktree/dirty legs stay bead-only — budget unchanged. R4 is a pure comment supersession, model() already resolved to family. No new ADR. Impacted domains (workflow, core) correct. Gate tests: complete/panel/config PASS; instruct's 581s hang is the pre-existing z4ps/7y4d bd-subprocess flake on a zero-diff package — not a regression.
findings: ADR-0037 preserved: internal/panel/gate.go zero-byte diff vs 45ae7577; PanelGateDecision remains the single decision home. R1's non-bead fix operates purely at the cmd/mindspec layer (fact-gathering via GateIO.RevParse closure nonBeadTargetRevParse + render-layer sanitizeNonBeadDecision, which never mutates d.Action).

ADR-0037 R3 gate field decision-inert and validated against the single config.PanelGateKeys enum (isValidPanelGateKey iterates the slice, no literal copy). TestPanel_GateFieldDecisionInertAllEnumValues pins Action/Message/ApproveThreshold identical across spec_approve/plan_approve/bead/final_review/adhoc/"" over Allow+Block scenarios.

ADR-0035 adhered: invalid --gate → guard.NewFailure with 5-key recovery line; rejectControlBytes runs before the enum check; non-bead tally Block recovery names a real re-panel command and drops the false complete-<bead> advice.

ADR-0040 adhered: L2 panel-verb surface made truthful for non-bead panels; L3 ms-panel.js hardened (R2 bare-$ fold) with ALLOWED_CLI/buildCommand chokepoint untouched and mirror byte-identical; R3 is the writer-side ratchet completion.

ADR-0030 adhered: non-bead staleness routes one rev-parse through revParseForPanelFn→executor seam; panelBeadWorktreePath returns "" for empty beadID so no bd worktree spawn; net subprocess count unchanged. cmd/mindspec is not an enforcement package so the boundary lint does not even bind — plan correctly evaluates-but-does-not-cite.

No new ADR smuggled; this is bug-fix/writer-completion work. R4 (internal/config) is doc-comment-only supersession of 112's phrasing; validateReviewerEntries/Reviewer.model() logic unchanged, resolve-to-family already the behavior.
Impacted domains workflow (cmd/mindspec, internal/panel, ms-panel.js mirror) + core (internal/config) declared correctly and match the ownership files.

No regression to the complete gate (internal/complete PASS) or instruct panel-state: both packages carry a zero-byte diff vs main, making their behavior byte-identical by construction — the R1 consistency fence holds NET on the branch.

NON-BLOCKING: internal/instruct test run hits a 581s timeout hang with an os/exec/bd goroutine dump — the known pre-existing z4ps/7y4d env-dependent flake (TestRun_IdleNoBeads). Given the zero-byte diff to internal/instruct it cannot be a spec-113 regression; BRIEF explicitly excepts it. internal/panel and internal/config PASS.
carried_forward: round-1 APPROVE carried: lens (spec-goal/grounding/R3-inertness/ADR/doc-artifacts) unaffected by the G2 render-layer escaping fix + .beads data regen
